sites.name: Barrens to Bald Eagle Corridor
sites.desc: A critical landscape-scale corridor connecting the Scotia Barrens to the Bald Eagle Ridge. This project protects migration routes for black bears, bobcats, and forest interior songbirds across fragmented habitat.

sites.name: Buck Run
sites.desc: A headwater stream restoration area in northern Centre County. Buck Run flows through reclaimed forest land, supporting native brook trout populations and riparian habitat recovery.

sites.name: Buffalo Run Farm
sites.desc: A working farm implementing best management practices for water quality. Streambank fencing, cover crops, and nutrient management plans protect Buffalo Run, a key tributary of Spring Creek.

sites.name: Canoe Creek Conservation Bank
sites.desc: A wetland and stream mitigation bank near the Canoe Creek watershed. This site generates conservation credits by restoring and permanently protecting degraded habitats, offsetting impacts from development elsewhere in the region.

sites.name: Dry Hollow
sites.desc: A stream and hollow restoration project addressing acid mine drainage and habitat degradation. Dry Hollow supports passive treatment systems that restore water quality and aquatic life in formerly impaired waterways.

sites.name: Gailbraith Gap
sites.desc: A mountain gap property protecting contiguous forest along Tussey Mountain. The site safeguards headwater streams, provides critical flyover habitat for migratory raptors, and maintains the integrity of the Ridge and Valley landscape.

sites.name: Gray's Woods
sites.desc: A remnant old-growth forest fragment within the Spring Creek watershed. Gray's Woods harbors mature oak-hickory stands and provides critical urban forest ecosystem services including stormwater management and carbon sequestration.

sites.name: Meyer Dairy
sites.desc: A dairy farm partnering in conservation easement programs to protect productive farmland and water resources. Riparian buffers and rotational grazing practices reduce nutrient runoff into local waterways.

sites.name: Millbrook Marsh
sites.desc: One of Centre County's premier natural areas, Millbrook Marsh is a calcareous fen and wetland complex fed by limestone springs. A boardwalk trail system provides access to this ecologically significant site, home to rare plants and diverse bird species.

sites.name: Musser Gap
sites.desc: A popular multi-use trail corridor in the Rothrock State Forest. Musser Gap connects mountain biking, hiking, and equestrian trails while protecting forested slopes and seasonal stream headwaters.

sites.name: Nittany Noll
sites.desc: A scenic hilltop preserve overlooking Nittany Valley. Features mixed hardwood forests, wildflower meadows, and panoramic views of Mount Nittany and the surrounding ridgelines.

sites.name: Plummers Hollow
sites.desc: A pristine mountain hollow preserve in the Bald Eagle Ridge. Plummers Hollow is a biodiversity hotspot featuring old-growth hemlock stands, rich fern understories, and documented populations of rare moths and salamanders.

sites.name: Rhoneymeade
sites.desc: A former arboretum and estate now managed as a public nature preserve. Rhoneymeade features curated tree collections, naturalized meadows, and limestone spring habitats along the Penns Valley corridor.

sites.name: Rockview
sites.desc: Adjacent to the historic Rockview facility, this conservation area encompasses wetlands, grasslands, and early-successional habitat. The site serves as an important corridor connecting natural areas in the Spring Creek watershed.

sites.name: Rupert Cave
sites.desc: A significant karst feature in Huntingdon County supporting bat hibernacula and unique subterranean ecosystems. Conservation efforts focus on protecting cave-dwelling species and maintaining hydrological connectivity in the limestone landscape.

sites.name: Thompson Woods
sites.desc: A forested tract south of State College featuring mixed hardwoods and seasonal wetland pockets. Thompson Woods provides habitat connectivity and recreational trail opportunities in the growing urban-wildland interface.
